1
00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:07,200
Script Title: The Collateral Damage — A Small Business Owner vs. the Machinery of Trade War

2
00:00:07,200 --> 00:00:15,280
Total Estimated Length: 20 minutes. Delivery Pace: measured, contemplative narration with natural

3
00:00:15,280 --> 00:00:24,480
pauses for reflection. Hook 0:00 to 0:45 | approximately 45 seconds. Visual

4
00:00:24,480 --> 00:00:34,399
atmosphere: slow motion footage of cargo ships slicing through Pacific fog at dawn. Cut to a single woman

5
00:00:34,399 --> 00:00:41,359
in a Virginia workshop stitching fabric by lamplight. Distant thunder of political speeches.

6
00:00:41,359 --> 00:00:50,159
In the long arc of civilizations, empires do not fall with a single cataclysm. They erode through

7
00:00:50,240 --> 00:00:58,160
the quiet accumulation of decisions that seemed, at the time, entirely rational. Tonight we descend

8
00:00:58,160 --> 00:01:05,040
not into the marble halls of Rome or the vanished courts of the Maya, but into something more intimate:

9
00:01:05,760 --> 00:01:12,719
the collateral damage of great-power competition. This is the story of Sarah Wells — a mother,

10
00:01:12,719 --> 00:01:19,760
an inventor, an entrepreneur — and how the invisible architecture of global trade suddenly

11
00:01:19,760 --> 00:01:26,240
turned against the very people it was meant to serve.

12
00:01:26,240 --> 00:01:33,520
Background and the Unfolding of Systemic Fragility — 0:45 to 8:00 | approximately 7 minutes 15 seconds. Visual

13
00:01:33,520 --> 00:01:41,360
atmosphere: archival-style reenactments — kitchen table in 2012 with baby gear, factory floors in

14
00:01:41,360 --> 00:01:48,160
China, loading docks in 2025, congressional hearing room. Intercut with graphs of tariff

15
00:01:48,159 --> 00:01:55,840
escalations and supply chain maps glowing red. Sarah Wells began in the most ordinary of places

16
00:01:56,479 --> 00:02:03,599
— her own kitchen in Fairfax County, Virginia. Like so many founders before her, she saw a gap no one

17
00:02:03,599 --> 00:02:10,639
else had filled. Working mothers needed a better way to carry breast pumps, bottles, ice packs —

18
00:02:10,640 --> 00:02:18,800
dignity and function in one elegant bag. In 2012 she started stitching prototypes. By the mid-2010s

19
00:02:18,800 --> 00:02:26,640
Sarah Wells Bags had found its rhythm: innovative design in America, precision manufacturing

20
00:02:26,640 --> 00:02:34,320
in China, where entire ecosystems of skilled labor, specialized materials, and ruthless efficiency

21
00:02:34,319 --> 00:02:41,519
had coalesced over decades. For years the system worked with deceptive smoothness. Containers crossed

22
00:02:41,519 --> 00:02:48,319
the Pacific. Margins held. American mothers received thoughtfully designed products at prices

23
00:02:48,319 --> 00:02:54,719
they could afford. The complexity remained hidden, like the root systems beneath a thriving forest.

24
00:02:55,759 --> 00:03:02,639
Then, in early 2025, the winds shifted. As geopolitical tensions hardened into policy,

25
00:03:03,280 --> 00:03:10,800
tariffs on Chinese imports climbed — first one layer, then another. Sarah's shipment was already at sea

26
00:03:10,800 --> 00:03:16,800
when the second round hit. By the time the containers reached port, her clearing costs

27
00:03:16,800 --> 00:03:25,200
had ballooned by $15,000 on a single order. Cumulative duties reached levels approaching 54

28
00:03:25,840 --> 00:03:33,120
to 62 percent. She faced an impossible trinity: raise prices and lose loyal customers already

29
00:03:33,120 --> 00:03:41,040
stretched by inflation; absorb the costs and bleed cash; or attempt reshoring, only to discover that

30
00:03:41,040 --> 00:03:47,520
American manufacturing capacity for her specialized components simply did not exist at scale.

31
00:03:47,520 --> 00:03:55,360
Raw materials, even for "American-made" assembly, still traced back through Chinese supply chains.

32
00:03:55,360 --> 00:04:03,360
The cost multiplier: three to four times. This was not failure of nerve or innovation. It was the

33
00:04:03,360 --> 00:04:09,920
sudden revelation of institutional fragility — the brittle interdependence built into just-

34
00:04:09,920 --> 00:04:16,879
in-time global systems. Small businesses, which employ nearly half of American workers,

35
00:04:16,879 --> 00:04:22,879
operate with thin margins and limited buffers. Large corporations can hedge currencies,

36
00:04:23,439 --> 00:04:31,040
renegotiate contracts, absorb shocks, or pass costs downstream. The Sarah Wellses of the economy

37
00:04:31,040 --> 00:04:40,159
cannot. Across 2025, business bankruptcies climbed toward fifteen-year highs. Cash-flow crises rippled

38
00:04:40,160 --> 00:04:47,120
through retail, manufacturing, and services. What looked on spreadsheets like necessary medicine

39
00:04:47,120 --> 00:04:54,800
for national security revealed itself, on the ground, as a wealth transfer mechanism: from small-

40
00:04:54,800 --> 00:05:02,800
scale creators and their communities upward toward those with scale, capital, and political access.

41
00:05:02,800 --> 00:05:09,840
History offers echoes. The Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act of 1930 was passed with similar intentions

42
00:05:10,400 --> 00:05:18,000
to shield American farmers and industry amid economic pain. Instead, retaliatory tariffs

43
00:05:18,000 --> 00:05:24,319
collapsed global trade by two-thirds within years. What began as protection became mutual

44
00:05:24,319 --> 00:05:31,680
impoverishment. The intricate web of commerce, once severed, does not heal quickly. Supply

45
00:05:31,680 --> 00:05:38,079
chains are not mere logistics; they are living ecologies of trust, skill, and incremental knowledge

46
00:05:38,079 --> 00:05:44,639
that take generations to grow and moments to disrupt.

47
00:05:44,639 --> 00:05:50,480
Surviving Assets & Irreducible Value — 8:00 to 13:00 | approximately 5 minutes. Visual atmosphere:

48
00:05:51,280 --> 00:05:58,879
close-ups of hands working — stitching, sketching, customer emails. Archival footage of resilient

49
00:05:58,879 --> 00:06:06,319
small-town businesses through past crises. Subtle data visualizations showing adaptive

50
00:06:06,319 --> 00:06:14,159
small firms.
